text:
Lighthouse is a Unity application framework
built around scene management at its core.
It provides a complete foundation for game development
and accelerates your product.

You can focus on creating your game content.

Scene transitions, dialogs, localization, and more —
essential but non-core aspects of your game
are comprehensively covered by Lighthouse.

With code generation, templates, and presets, you're up and running from day one.

There are two key advantages to adopting this framework for professional use.

1. A ready-to-use foundation
Scene management, screen stack, UI animation, and localization are available immediately.
Managed entirely through DI, it makes your product customizable "at any time."

This eliminates engineer-dependent bottlenecks in your workflow,
reduces step-level dependencies,
and enables faster development planning and smooth onboarding when your team grows.

2. Consistent quality regardless of team skill level
Lighthouse provides lifecycle structure and discipline.
Even junior engineers can implement with confidence,
while senior engineers can adjust those boundaries to fit their product.

There is one advantage to adopting this framework as an indie developer.

"Wanting to make your original game, but wasting time on things that don't matter" — that ends.
Scene management, dialogs, localization — Lighthouse handles it all.
